name: Rubi, Serra de Can Riquet, Fuente Avellaneda - circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Rubí offers diverse landscapes and a network of trails suitable for various skill levels. The region features hilly terrain, including areas like Serra de Galliners and Serra de Can Guilera, with forests and valleys. The Llobregat River also provides opportunities for waterside routes. These natural features contribute to varied topography ideal for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Rubí The most popular mountain bike trail is Llobregat River – Llobregat River Footbridge loop from Rubí Centre, a 39.8 miles (64.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 43 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Llobregat River, offering a generally flat and accessible ride. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Rubi, Serra de Can Riquet, Fuente Avellaneda - circular, a moderate 14.5 miles (23.3 km) path. This route explores the Serra de Can Riquet, featuring varied terrain and natural surroundings. Local mountain bikers also love the Terres de Ca n'Oriol – Serra de Can Riquet Trail loop from Rubí Centre, a 27.1 miles (43.6 km) trail leading through hilly terrain and the Serra de Can Riquet, often completed in about 4 hours. Mountain biking around Rubí is defined by hilly terrain, forests, and river paths along the Llobregat. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. The routes in Rubí are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 1,400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Rubí's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Rubí?
A: Rubí offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 30 routes available on komoot. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging excursions.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Rubí?
A: Yes, Rubí has options for beginners. There are 4 easy mountain bike routes, often featuring less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them suitable for those new to the sport or looking for a relaxed ride. The region's diverse trails ensure there's something for everyone.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Rubí?
A: Mountain biking in Rubí features diverse terrain, including hilly landscapes, forests, and valleys, particularly around areas like Serra de Galliners and Serra de Can Guilera. You'll also find routes along the Llobregat River, offering more accessible, generally flatter paths. Some areas, like Serra de Can Guilera, are known for scenic singletrack trails, and Rubike Naturpark provides a dynamic riding experience with opportunities for freeriding.

Q: What options are there for experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: For experienced mountain bikers, Rubí offers 10 difficult routes that demand advanced riding skills and very good fitness. These trails often feature significant elevation changes and more technical sections. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19399447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Terres de Ca n'Oriol – Serra de Can Riquet Trail loop from Rubí Centre&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 27.1 miles with over 800 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Rubí?
A: While mountain biking in Rubí, you can explore various natural features and landmarks. The trails often traverse the hilly terrain of Serra de Galliners and Serra de Can Guilera, offering scenic views. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rierada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4482632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallvidrera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5109965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portell de Valldaura Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The Llobregat River also provides opportunities for waterside rides.

Q: Are there many circular mountain bike routes in Rubí?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Rubí are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952755720/rubi-serra-de-can-riquet-fuente-avellaneda-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rubi, Serra de Can Riquet, Fuente Avellaneda - circular&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the Serra de Can Riquet, and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/16329577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Roig – Turó de les Martines loop from Rubí Centre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Rubí?
A: The mountain biking trails in Rubí are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the extensive network of trails, and the variety of options available for different skill levels, from river paths to challenging hilly terrain.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Rubí?
A: Rubí, like much of Spain, offers excellent conditions for mountain biking almost year-round due to its favorable climate. While specific seasonal considerations can vary, generally spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures for riding. Even in winter, conditions are often mild enough to enjoy the trails.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Rubí?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always explicit, the presence of easy and moderate routes suggests suitable options. Trails along the Llobregat River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7107499" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobregat River – Llobregat River Footbridge loop from Rubí Centre&lt;/a&gt;, are generally moderate and accessible, making them good choices for families with some riding experience.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that follow the Llobregat River?
A: Yes, the Llobregat River offers several mountain bike routes. These trails typically provide a generally flatter and more accessible riding experience compared to the hilly 'Serra' areas. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31629639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobregat River – Pont dels Molls loop from Rubí Centre&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate route covering nearly 77 kilometers.

Q: Where can I find mountain biking areas with a more informal or dynamic riding experience in Rubí?
A: For a more informal and dynamic riding experience, you might consider Rubike Naturpark. Described as a fun local zone primarily for mountain biking, it allows for freeriding and creating jumps and trails, suggesting a less structured environment for riders looking to experiment and develop skills. This area is considered a municipal park.
tips: Good route for a Sunday afternoon
It is a track that follows the crest of the Serra de Can Riquet. It's long but not too difficult and has a lot of nice views. It is an alternative off-road path from Castellbisbal to Ullastrell.
From here you have good views of Monserrate. It seems much closer than it is.
A fast, narrow and fun path. It is not difficult and you can do it in both directions.

waypoints.name: Singletrack of the Font d'Avellanosa of the Torrent de Can Roig
